title: Warn when setting healthcheck in OCI format
body:
Signed-off-by: Ashley Cui <acui@redhat.com>

#### What type of PR is this?
/kind other

#### What this PR does / why we need it:
Healthcheck only works in Docker format, so when healthcheck is set in OCI format, warn user that it will be discarded
#### How to verify it

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:
Fixes: https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/2388

comments.body:
LGTM
@cevich have you been looking at the Buildah CI errors?  I keep seeing:

```
[+0081s]  * 99f46263 "Merge #2463" ... FAIL
[+0081s]   - FAIL - does not have a valid DCO
```
LGTM
@ashley-cui Please rebase and repush to see if it fixed ci.
bors r+
:clock1: Waiting for PR status (Github check) to be set, probably by CI. Bors will automatically try to run when all required PR statuses are set.

> @cevich have you been looking at the Buildah CI errors? I keep seeing:  [DCO error]

Assuming you're seeing this on branches?  This is the thing I'm trying to fix.  If you're seeing it in PRs, please let me know ASAP.
bors retry

Build failed (retrying...):
  * cirrus-ci/success

I'm babysitting the bors job here, re-running gate to see if I can help it pass.  It's hitting the golangci-lint ***ambiguous-death*** problem:

```
[+0017s] ./tests/tools/build/golangci-lint run --deadline=20m --color=always -j1
[+0128s] make: *** [Makefile:171: lint] Killed
```

I'm hoping some re-runs might help.  Otherwise, we'll need to wait for #2625
bors retry
Already running a review

The git_validate tool is now reporting:

```
[+0072s] + exec git-validation -v -run DCO,short-subject -range 2b32b824f81b4397f3082ed284d26f7e04b380a8..HEAD
[+0073s] time="2020-09-16T08:30:49-04:00" level=error msg="mm[git] cmd: \"git --no-pager log --pretty=format:%H 2b32b824f81b4397f3082ed284d26f7e04b380a8..HEAD\""
[+0073s] 2020/09/16 08:30:49 exit status 128
```

(before it was 125 IIRC).  Despite/either case I have no idea what that exit code means.  Fixing and re-fixing this is getting very tedious, and force-merging risks allowing actual problems into `master`.  I think it's time we seek some help from the `git-validation` maintainers, clearly something is very badly broken.
Opened https://github.com/vbatts/git-validation/issues/46

well....some good news:  I'm able to reproduce the problem with:

```
$ git checkout upstream/staging
$ export GITVALIDATE_EPOCH=2b32b824f81b4397f3082ed284d26f7e04b380a8
$ make validate
make -C tests/tools
make[1]: Entering directory '/home/cevich/devel/buildah/tests/tools'
make[1]: Nothing to be done for 'all'.
make[1]: Leaving directory '/home/cevich/devel/buildah/tests/tools'
+ exec git-validation -q -run DCO,short-subject -range 2b32b824f81b4397f3082ed284d26f7e04b380a8..HEAD
fatal: invalid object name 'gpg'.
ERRO[0000] [git] cmd: "git --no-pager log -1 --pretty=format:%aE gpg: Signature made Wed 16 Sep 2020 08:26:07 AM EDT"
2020/09/16 09:57:47 exit status 128
make: *** [Makefile:97: validate] Error 1
```

Build failed (retrying...):
  * cirrus-ci/success

Build failed:
  * cirrus-ci/success

bors retry 

(is this the right move?)

I think this is the fix (it works for me): https://github.com/vbatts/git-validation/pull/47

Is there a way we can temporarily point buildah at my branch until @vbatts can merge? (assuming it's the right fix)

Build failed:
  * cirrus-ci/success

Build failed:
  * cirrus-ci/success

Do I need to rebase or anything? 
bors retry

@ashley-cui sometimes when bors gets this painful, I make a small change in my sandbox.  I then do git add and `git commit --amend -s`, then remove the change, do those again, fetch, rebase and push and it often times clears things.

You shouldn't need to do that, but I have found that sometime a well done git dance appeases the bors monster.  YMMV.

Build failed:
  * cirrus-ci/success
bors retry

> You shouldn't need to do that

The key to bors is remembering/understanding what it's doing:

    *Test your PR possibly in combination with other PRs, as if it/they had already merged into master.*

So clearly there are several ways it can break, depending on what's currently in it's merge queue, or (like in this case) what's broken with testing branches.  When the underlying failure cause is understood, most fixes become more purposeful and less guesswork :grin:

Granted, figuring out the root-cause is arguably difficult for many people, so please feel free to ping me for help.

[Most recent bors failure:](https://github.com/containers/buildah/runs/1128199407)

```
[+0066s] make[1]: Leaving directory '/var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah/tests/tools'
[+0067s] + exec git-validation -v -run DCO,short-subject -range 2b32b824f81b4397f3082ed284d26f7e04b380a8..HEAD
[+0067s] time="2020-09-17T07:21:44-04:00" level=error msg="mm[git] cmd: \"git --no-pager log --pretty=format:%H 2b32b824f81b4397f3082ed284d26f7e04b380a8..HEAD\""
[+0067s] 2020/09/17 07:21:44 exit status 128
[+0067s] make: *** [Makefile:97: validate] Error 1
[07:21:44] END - [+0067s] total duration since START
```

We're still dying from what *appears* like the problem I fixed.  So either we're not getting the new git-validate version, or something new is breaking.  This is where my knowledge of golang falls short: How do we determine what git-validate is being used?  I saw reference to a `1.0.0` version in a `go.mod` somewherre...could that be to blame?

Build failed:
  * cirrus-ci/success

Debugging hint: Bors does it's job using one of two special branches:  `staging` and `trying`.  For normal use (i.e. not a `bors try`) the first one is being used.  This branch can be checked out locally, and `make validate` run to help see what the problem might be...

```
$ git remote update
$ git checkout upstream/staging
$ make clean
$ make validate
make -C tests/tools
make[1]: Entering directory '/home/cevich/devel/buildah/tests/tools'
make[1]: Nothing to be done for 'all'.
make[1]: Leaving directory '/home/cevich/devel/buildah/tests/tools'
+ exec git-validation -q -run DCO,short-subject -range 2b32b824f81b4397f3082ed284d26f7e04b380a8..HEAD
fatal: invalid object name 'gpg'.
ERRO[0000] [git] cmd: "git --no-pager log -1 --pretty=format:%GS gpg: Signature made Thu 17 Sep 2020 07:17:11 AM EDT"
2020/09/17 07:45:01 exit status 128
make: *** [Makefile:97: validate] Error 1
```

This is for-sure the old problem, so (somehow) the latest version of git-validate is ***not*** being used.  How do we fix that?
Fix should be in https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/2632

fingers crossed

bors retry

Build failed:
  * cirrus-ci/success
Apparently you didn't cross your fingers enough @ashley-cui .  @cevich thoughts?
I think she needs to rebase and try again.

> I think she needs to rebase and try again.

@rhatdan already rebased, still failing

> Apparently you didn't cross your fingers enough @ashley-cui . @cevich thoughts?

This is a new problem.  Tests are failing when attempting to merge this PR, and run tests:

* https://github.com/containers/buildah/runs/1130519562
* https://github.com/containers/buildah/runs/1130519563

Not sure if those are flakes or not (haven't dug into them yet).  I suspect if you re-ran those same tests enough in the PR, they would also fail.

In both cases, it's different tests failing, but they share a common/similar error message:

```# open state file /proc/108396/stat: No such process```
bors retry

Build failed (retrying...):
  * cirrus-ci/success

Build succeeded:
  * cirrus-ci/success
